basics.name: Maya W. Mikelson
basics.Research Interests: Conflict and cooperation, security studies, militarization, international interventions, human rights

education.area: High Honors in Political Science, minor in Data Science

work.name: Inter-university Consortium for Political and Social Research
highlights: Developed latent variable models in R with the Stan library to calculate the likelihood of signing treaties in every year between 1966 and 2022.
Coded a web scraper to analyze treaty ratification data from the UN Treaty Body database.

work.name: Ralph Bunche Summer Insitute (RBSI)
work.summary: Professors Paula D. McClain and David A. Siegel
highlights: Developed independent research project on the impact of third-party military interventions on human rights and government repression during intrastate conflict using a Heckman Probit Selection Model.
Completed graduate-level courses in Race and American Politics and Probability and Regression.

work.name: NSF REU Accountability, Behavior & Conflict in Democratic Politics
highlights: Developed an independent empirical project analyzing the relationship between militarization and interstate conflict that was presented in a poster session at APSA 2023, Los Angeles.
Compiled historical sources into case studies examining race riots and police violence in the United States.

awards.title: Nominated for Sweetland Prize for Excellence in Upper-Level Writing

projects.name: Measuring Militarization and the Link with Interstate Conflict
highlights: Senior Honors Thesis, University of Michigan, Spring 2025
Poster presentation at the Midwest Political Science Association Conference, April 2025
Poster presentation at the American Political Science Association Conference, September 2025

projects.name: Third-Party Military Interventions, Government Repression, and Human Rights
highlights: Ralph Bunche Summer Institute Paper, June 2024

projects.name: Militarization and Interstate Armed Conflict
highlights: Poster presentation at the American Political Science Association Annual Meeting, September 2023
